title: atropa indobelladonna
intro: Atropa indobelladonna, also known as the Indian belladonna or bhallataka, is a plant species of the deadly nightshade family, containing tropane alkaloids, particularly scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which are known for their psychoactive and medicinal properties. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to reduce spasms, relieve pain, and act as a local anesthetic. The health benefits of Atropa indobelladonna include its use in treating various conditions such as gastrointestinal disorders, epilepsy, and snake bites, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and act as an anti-inflammatory. Atropa indobelladonna can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including tinctures, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used both externally and internally to harness its medicinal properties.
